what_it_does: Defines a React info-panel component for a virtual-hub apartment view, plus a small helper that renders inline SVG icons from arrays of path strings.
observations: Two functional React components: FeatIcon and InfoPanel.
FeatIcon maps an array of path 'd' strings into <path> elements inside a 24x24 SVG.
InfoPanel renders an <aside> with a close button, title fallback, four hardcoded feature icons (2 BED, LIVING, 2 BATH, 1 CAR), a placeholder description block, and an enquiry CTA.
Uses optional chaining on hub?.title and a literal fallback title.
Assigns window.InfoPanel = InfoPanel at the end.
Class names use a consistent 'vh-info-' prefix.
No prop-types, TypeScript, or external imports are present.
standouts: Feature icons are authored as inline path-string arrays rather than imported assets.
The component mixes presentation with placeholder/fallback content.
It exposes itself on window, suggesting a non-module or legacy runtime target.
conventions: React functional components with destructured props.
Optional chaining for defensive prop access.
concerns: Description text is Lorem ipsum placeholder, not dynamic.
Feature icons and labels are hardcoded, not driven by hub data.
Close button has an aria-label, but the icon-only feature items lack accessible text.
CTA button has no onClick or href, so it is currently inert.
Global window assignment may conflict in module-based builds.
notes: A hand-rolled, closeable apartment info panel with inline SVG icon helpers and placeholder content, exposed globally as window.InfoPanel.
questions: What is the full shape of the hub prop beyond title?
Are the four feature icons meant to be fixed for every apartment, or should they be data-driven?
Is the window assignment intentional for a non-bundled environment?
What action should the 'Enquire about this apartment' button trigger?
purpose_vs_actual: The file matches the purpose of displaying apartment details in a side panel, but it currently relies on hardcoded feature icons, placeholder description, and a fallback title rather than fully dynamic data.
apparent_themes: Minimal prop-driven shell with baked-in sample content.
intention_signals: To give users a quick, dismissible summary of an apartment—its key features, description, and a next-step enquiry action—within a virtual-hub experience.
novelty: A compact icon-rendering DSL where SVG paths are passed as plain string arrays, making icons editable directly in component code.
contribution: A reusable info-panel layout shell with a close interaction, an inline-SVG icon helper pattern, and a clear real-estate feature-list structure that a fused system could adopt or parameterize.
summary_for_embedding: InfoPanel.jsx defines a React apartment-info side panel with a custom inline-SVG icon helper, hardcoded feature glyphs, placeholder description, and an enquiry CTA.
intention_for_embedding: To present apartment details and an enquiry call-to-action inside a virtual-hub UI.

symbols.does: Renders an inline SVG from an array of path 'd' strings.
symbols.description: A small presentational helper that receives an array of SVG path definitions and maps them into <path> elements inside a fixed 24x24 viewBox <svg>; used by InfoPanel to draw feature glyphs.

symbols.does: Renders a closable apartment information panel with feature icons, description, and enquiry CTA.
symbols.description: A React component accepting onClose and hub props; it renders an <aside> containing a close button, a title with a fallback string, four hardcoded feature icon blocks, a placeholder description section, and an enquiry button, then assigns itself to window.InfoPanel.
